briefing.headline: Rig up a Shakyhead — finesse spawn approach for stained water.
briefing.gameplan:
The Shakyhead is a strong choice for spawn with stained water and post frontal conditions.

Palaniuk: 'Extremely cold (42 degrees or below) and warmer than 90 degrees are prime conditions for a shaky-head. At cold extremes, fish are just up shallow beginning to get aggressive but not ready to eat a jig. A shaky-head is a small weedless finesse bait that's easy for lethargic fish to eat.'

Key adjustment for post frontal: slow down significantly — extended pauses, subtle movements, finesse presentations.

primaryApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, laydown. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

alternateApproach.retrieve: Palaniuk: 'Bass realize they can either chase prey and burn energy, or chill out in the shade, burn less energy and eat less. A shaky-head is a good way to pick off bigger fish out of brush piles that
alternateApproach.targets: Focus on flat, grass, laydown. Work the transitions and edges where structure changes.

proInsights.insight: Wheeler: The Punch Out Craw has five manifestations - full glide bait on 4/0 hook and 1/2-oz sinker, separated claws for football jig or Biffle Hardhead, trimmed small craw for swim jig/flipping jig/chatterbait/shakyhead, shortened torso for even ...

KVD: 'For all four finesse techniques (drop-shot, Neko, shaky head, Ned): 10lb braid to 8lb fluorocarbon leader with FG knot as baseline. Go heavier for cover or colored water. Longer 7-2 to 7-4 rods for open water long casts, shorter 6-10 to 7-fo...

Best for pitching to isolated cover - docks, logs, laydowns, boulders, stumps. A little tough to sink into thick cover. Effective on both smallmouth and largemouth, spawning, cruising, and fry guarding fish
Hackney: 'At Stick Marsh with no underwater vegetation, fish were spawning around willow trees and cane on the shoreline - they just wanted to have something to spawn around.'

briefing.depthStrategy: Post-frontal spawning bass become extremely lockjaw but won't abandon beds. Fish are still in 2-5ft but much harder to trigger. Slow way down and make repeated presentations to visible beds.
adjustIf: If skies cloud up and wind picks up, re-introduce moving baits — the reprieve from high pressure activates fish
If water clears significantly, downsize presentations and move to natural color patterns
If you get followers without commits, add scent or downsize your hook/weight
